checks.a_analysis: The output contains text that is directly traceable to the source PDF. No fabricated sections, introductions, or conclusions were found. All headings and paragraphs align with the source material.
The source contains one URL on page 5. While this URL is omitted in the output, no new or false contact information was generated.

All content-heavy pages (1-12) are represented in the output. Page 13 of the source contains only a footer fragment and a mislabeled page number, and its absence does not constitute a loss of meaningful section content.

The tables on pages 10, 11, and 12 were examined. All tables in the output contain the same number of data rows as the source PDF. For example, the status table on page 10 has rows 1-5, and the budget tables on page 12 are fully populated.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The conversion failed to capture significant hierarchical list data. On page 5, the defining sub-bullets for Single-factor and Two-factor authentication ('Something you know', 'Something you have', etc.) are entirely missing. On page 8, under the 'Potential Participants' section, every single sub-item (listing the specific state departments) is omitted, which accounts for over 70% of that section's content. Additionally, the reference URL on page 5 is missing from the converted text.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 8 lists sub-departments under 8 main headings (e.g., under 'VPN (Administrator Access)', it lists 'Department of Information Technology', 'Department of Public Safety', etc.). Page 5 includes sub-bullets for authentication types and a URL 'http://www.ncleg.net/Sessions/2015/Bills/House/PDF/H97v9.pdf'.
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 8, reading_order 52: Lists only the 8 top-level categories, omitting all 24 sub-items. Section 5, reading_orders 19-21: The URL is missing between the words 'See' and 'for reference.'.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections 5 and 8, corresponding to pages 5 and 8

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Excluding the significant omissions noted in C5, the text wording accurately reflects the source. Numerical values in the budget tables and dates in the plan tables were verified to be correct.

checks.a_analysis: The content in the output JSON sequentially follows the source document without any duplication of text blocks or paragraphs across sections.

The document's heading hierarchy is maintained correctly. H1 is used for titles (Establishing A Multi-Factor Authentication Solution, Contents), H2 for major sections (Legislative Request, Introduction, Background, etc.), and H3 for subsections (Goals). No levels are skipped, and the nesting is logical.

The tables on pages 10, 11, and 12 are structurally sound and match the source. For example, the Plan table on page 10 correctly captures the three-column structure for the 'Procurement Phase' row while maintaining two columns for the preceding 'Complete' rows. The budget section, while formatted as a table in the output for accessibility, accurately reflects the row-column relationship of categories and amounts shown in the source.
The reading order follows the logical flow of the original PDF, progressing from the title page through contents to the final budget tables.

There are significant issues with list conversion where nested sub-items (second-level bullets) are omitted entirely, flattening the structure and losing information. This occurs on Page 5 under 'Types of authentication' and on Page 8 under 'Potential Participants'.
checks.b_evidence_source:

o Department of Transportation
checks.b_evidence_location: Potential Participants section, page 8, element index 52

checks.a_analysis: A critical government URL present on page 5 is entirely missing from the output text. The text surrounding it ('See' and 'for reference.') is present, but the URL itself is not.
checks.b_evidence_location: Legislative Request section, page 5, elements 19 and 21

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the visual organization and logical flow of the original PDF document. Sections such as the Legislative Request, Introduction, Background, Benefits, and The MFA Project follow the same sequential order as the source. Information is organized in a single-column layout that accurately reflects the source's intent and makes the document easy to navigate. Although the PDF is fixed-layout and the HTML is responsive, the hierarchy of headings and the placement of information blocks are consistent.

The budget information from page 12 of the PDF, which uses alignment to present data, has been converted into well-structured HTML tables. The 'Plan' status list from pages 10-11 is also rendered as a table. These tables are visually clear, with distinct columns and rows, and the data remains perfectly readable and aligned. There are no instances of overlapping text or missing borders that would impede understanding.

The primary meaningful image from the source, the 'NC Information Technology' logo on the title page, is correctly present and rendered in the HTML output. No other meaningful images or charts from the source appear to be missing.

There is no evidence of body content duplication. Paragraphs, tables, and lists appear once in their respective sections. The inclusion of the document title and table of contents in both the navigation sidebar and the main body is an intentional accessibility feature and not a defect of content duplication.
